rationale: The answer correctly wakes up to the WAE/control-allocation issue: this is not just a prompt or wording problem but an unauthorized external refund commitment. It assigns approval as a hard workflow constraint, narrows agent judgment to eligibility/explanation/case creation, and moves the evidence gate before customer-facing commitment by requiring approved status, approval ID, and approved actor before refund language can be sent. It explicitly says prompt changes are insufficient and adds audits/regression tests. No fail signal of only tuning the prompt.
